description: Verify a DFN file. Before importing fractures from a DFN file, one should use the fracture verify-file command to check the validity/characteristics of the fractures represented in the file. The following quantities are displayed:

keywords.name: file
keywords.description: Specify the DFN file to verify.

keywords.name: fracman
keywords.description: Specify the file format. If no format is specified, then the file extension of the name specified with the file keyword is used to determine the file format. A ‘.dat’ file extension is assumed to be in the itasca format, a ‘.flab’ file extension is assumed to be in the fishlab format, and a ‘.fab’ file extension is assumed to be in the fracman format. The itasca format is as specified in DFN-Related File Formats , and is the default format. The fishlab format is the flowplane format used in FISHLab. The fracman format follows the FracMan ASCII output format.

keywords.name: property-file
keywords.description: Indicate the property file to be queried. See DFN-Related File Formats for a definition of the property file format.

keywords.name: file
keywords.description: Specify the DFN file to verify.
